youtube_items.title: [YT] The Loneliness Epidemic: How America Lost Its Community - YouTube
youtube_items.summary: There's a strange paradox in our modern world. We are connected to thousands of people online. Yet, many of us have never felt more alone. We live in crowded neighborhoods, but we don't know our neighbors names. This isn't just a feeling of sadness. It's an epidemic. The epidemic of loneliness, and it's eating away at the soul of America from the inside out. It wasn't always like this. America was built on a foundation of community. Life revolved around what sociologists call third places. The spaces outside of home and work where life actually happened. The church, the social club, the union hall, the local bar. That was the connective tissue that held society together. It's where friendships were forged, deals were made, and a real social safety net was formed, not by the government, but

youtube_items.title: [YT] How Declining Social Capital Is Driving the Loneliness Epidemic
youtube_items.summary: New research suggests that America has a loneliness problem. The 2024 American Social Capital Survey revealed a country longing for connection. Among its findings: 17% of Americans say they have no close friends. And another 18% say they have only one or two close friends. Even people with bigger friendship networks often see them weakened by distance. 44% of Americans say few or none of their friends are within a short distance. Despite that, Americans miss out on a lot of opportunities to connect with others. The study found that only 47% Americans speak with neighbors they don’t know. Only 44% say they attend events in their community. And only about 28% volunteer. Perhaps the study’s most provocative finding, however... ...was that the loneliness epidemic isn’t hitting all Americans eq

analysis.summary: Събитията във връзка с обществената структура и социалната позиция на хората включват по-голям интерес към политическите и външните връзки, но също и по-висок риск за социално изолиране и неравенство. Възможно е да се наблюдава устойчиво промяна в обществената динамика.
insights: Съществуват по-големи връзки между политически групи
youtube_insights: Разпространената тема на изолацията и социално неравенство
Съществуват по-големи връзки между политически групи
analysis.relevance_to_goal: Съществуват по-големи връзки между политически групи, но също се наблюдава по-високо неравенство и изолация. Това се отразява в обществената структура и възможно е да се наблюдава устойчиво промяна.
analysis.generalization: Съществуват по-големи връзки между политически групи, но също се наблюдава по-високо неравенство и изолация. Това се отразява в обществената структура и възможно е да се наблюдава устойчиво промяна.

raw_items.title: Methodology
raw_items.summary: The American Trends Panel survey methodology Overview Data in this report comes from Wave 192 of the American Trends Panel (ATP), Pew Research Center’s nationally representative panel of randomly selected U.S. adults. The survey was conducted April 20-26, 2026. A total of 5,103 panelists responded out of 5,898 who were sampled, for a survey-level response […]

raw_items.title: Democrats and Republicans place more importance on advancing own policies than finding common ground
raw_items.summary: Republicans and Republican leaning independents overwhelmingly (76%) say Trump is doing an excellent or good job pushing for his policies, regardless of whether Democratic officials agree with them. By comparison, Democrats and Democratic leaners continue to be more critical of the job their elected officials have done at pushing against Trump’s policies when they disagree. […]
